source: Bloons Wiki (bloons.fandom.com): Damage Types / Bloon Properties / Status Effects — cross-checked against the game-sourced immune_to fields in bloons.json, verified 2026-06-27
note: Curated damage-type + status-effect INTERACTION layer. The per-bloon immune_to lists in bloons.json are game-data-sourced and authoritative; this file adds the conceptual rules the model needs to reason about 'can tower X deal with bloon Y'. Each damage type's blocked_by_properties (excluding the status-only 'Frozen') is cross-checked against bloons.json by tests/unit/services/test_btd6_interaction_service.py, so a curated error or a future data drift fails CI. Crosspath examples are quoted from the game's own upgrade descriptions in stats/*.json.

damage_types.name: Normal
damage_types.summary: The catch-all damage type with NO bloon-property immunity — it pops Lead, Black, White, Purple, and Zebra alike (camo still needs detection). Many tier-5 attacks convert to Normal so they answer everything.
damage_types.pops: every bloon type (no damage-type immunity blocks it)

damage_types.name: Sharp
damage_types.summary: Standard projectile/blade damage — the default for most Primary monkeys (Dart, Boomerang, Tack, basic Ninja, Sniper). Cannot pop Lead or Frozen bloons without an upgrade that changes the damage type or adds lead-popping.
damage_types.pops: every bloon EXCEPT Lead and Frozen (Black, White, Purple, Zebra all take Sharp fine)

damage_types.name: Shatter
damage_types.summary: A close cousin of Sharp used by some shrapnel/glaive effects (e.g. the Desperado's Avenger slug shrapnel). Like Sharp it is blocked by Lead, so on its own it cannot pop Lead bloons or the lead-bearing DDT.
damage_types.pops: every bloon EXCEPT Lead (and the lead-bearing DDT)

damage_types.name: Explosion
damage_types.summary: Blast damage from Bomb Shooter, Mortar, Cannon, etc. Blocked ONLY by the Black property, so it cannot pop Black or Zebra bloons, nor the black-bearing DDT. It pops Lead bloons fine.
damage_types.pops: every bloon EXCEPT Black and Zebra (and the black-bearing DDT). Note: it DOES pop Lead.

damage_types.name: Cold
damage_types.summary: Ice Monkey's damage/freeze. Blocked by both White and Lead, so base Ice Monkey cannot pop or freeze White, Zebra, Lead, or DDT bloons. The Cold Snap crosspath (Ice 2-0-0) adds 'Can freeze and pop Lead and Camo Bloons'; Embrittlement (Ice 4-0-0) lets it hit MOAB-class and strips Lead immunity.
damage_types.pops: non-White, non-Lead bloons (so NOT White, Zebra, Lead, or DDT without the Cold Snap / Embrittlement crosspath)

damage_types.name: Glacier
damage_types.summary: A stronger cold variant that, unlike plain Cold, is NOT blocked by Lead — it can pop Lead bloons. It is still blocked by the White property.
damage_types.pops: every bloon EXCEPT White and Zebra. Unlike Cold, Glacier DOES pop Lead.

damage_types.name: Frigid
damage_types.summary: A niche cold sub-type blocked by both White and Purple (so also Zebra).
damage_types.pops: every bloon EXCEPT White, Purple, and Zebra

damage_types.name: Energy
damage_types.summary: Laser/beam damage from some Dartling, Sub, and Super Monkey upgrades. Blocked by Lead AND Purple, so it cannot pop Lead, Purple, or DDT bloons.
damage_types.pops: every bloon EXCEPT Lead and Purple (and the lead-bearing DDT)

damage_types.name: Plasma
damage_types.summary: Plasma damage (Super Monkey Plasma Blasts, Plasma Monkey Fan Club, etc.). Blocked only by Purple. It pops Lead, Black, White, and Zebra fine.
damage_types.pops: every bloon EXCEPT Purple. Note: it DOES pop Lead.

damage_types.name: Fire
damage_types.summary: Fire damage (e.g. Tack Shooter Ring of Fire, Wizard, Druid, Phoenix). Blocked only by Purple. It pops Lead and Black fine — a popular DDT answer.
damage_types.pops: every bloon EXCEPT Purple. Note: it DOES pop Lead and Black.

damage_types.name: Acid
damage_types.summary: Acid/corrosive damage (Alchemist, Bloon Dissolver glue, Acidic Mixture Dip). No standard bloon property blocks it, so it pops Lead, Black, White, Purple, and Zebra.
damage_types.pops: every standard bloon (Lead, Black, White, Purple, Zebra); only the DLC Glass bloon resists Acid

status_effects.name: Glue (slow)
status_effects.summary: Glue is a STATUS EFFECT, not a damage type, so it IGNORES damage-type immunity — it slows Lead, Black, Purple, and Zebra bloons alike. Lead does NOT resist glue. The only gate is MOAB-class: base glue cannot stick to MOAB-class blimps (MOAB/BFB/ZOMG/DDT/BAD) — you need MOAB Glue (Glue Gunner 0-0-3) to target and slow them. Glue Strike (Glue Gunner 0-4-0) does not pop bloons; it debuffs them to take more damage from all sources, so it needs another tower to deal the damage.
status_effects.lead: works fine — Lead resists damage types, not glue
status_effects.moab_class: needs MOAB Glue (Glue Gunner 0-0-3) to stick to MOAB-class; DDTs are gluable with MOAB Glue + camo detection
status_effects.bad: the BAD is immune to all slow/glue effects

status_effects.name: Slow / Freeze (Ice)
status_effects.summary: Ice Monkey's freeze/slow is COLD-based, so unlike glue it IS blocked by the Lead and White properties — base Ice Monkey cannot freeze or slow Lead, White, Zebra, or DDT bloons. Crosspaths fix this: Cold Snap (Ice 2-0-0) 'Can freeze and pop Lead and Camo Bloons'; Embrittlement (Ice 4-0-0) hits MOAB-class and strips Lead immunity; the Arctic Wind (Ice 0-3-0) slow aura is cold, so it does NOT slow Lead/DDT.
status_effects.lead: base ice does NOT affect Lead (cold is blocked by lead) — needs Cold Snap (2-0-0) or Embrittlement (4-0-0)
status_effects.moab_class: base ice cannot freeze MOAB-class; Embrittlement (4-0-0), Snowstorm (0-4-0), and Absolute Zero (0-5-0) are the upgrades that affect them
status_effects.bad: the BAD is immune to all freeze/slow effects

status_effects.name: Knockback / Blowback
status_effects.summary: Knockback pushes bloons backward; it is a status effect, not damage. Non-MOAB bloons are knocked back by many towers, but MOAB-class blimps need a dedicated MOAB-knockback upgrade — e.g. Heli Pilot MOAB Shove (0-0-3) — to be moved.
status_effects.moab_class: needs a MOAB-targeting knockback upgrade such as Heli Pilot MOAB Shove (0-0-3)
status_effects.bad: the BAD is immune to all knockback

status_effects.name: Stun
status_effects.summary: Stun freezes a bloon in place briefly; it is a status effect, not damage. Most MOAB-class bloons resist base stuns; the BAD is immune to all stuns except a handful of named abilities (e.g. Time Stop).
status_effects.moab_class: many base stuns do not affect MOAB-class; dedicated MOAB-stun upgrades exist
status_effects.bad: the BAD is immune to all stuns except a few named abilities (Time Stop, etc.)

pop_guide.needs: Explosion, Fire, Plasma, Glacier, Acid, or Normal damage
pop_guide.blocked: Sharp, Shatter, Cold, and Energy damage cannot pop Lead
pop_guide.note: Towers that pop Lead at base (verified from the game data) include Bomb Shooter and Mortar (explosion) and Alchemist (acid). A Sharp tower needs a damage-type-changing upgrade (e.g. Sniper's Full Metal Jacket 1-0-0 makes its shots Normal) or a lead-popping buff such as Monkey Intelligence Bureau (Village 0-3-0) or Alchemist's Acidic Mixture Dip.

pop_guide.needs: any NON-Explosion damage (Sharp, Fire, Plasma, Cold, Energy, Acid, Normal)
pop_guide.blocked: Explosion damage cannot pop Black (or Zebra) bloons
pop_guide.note: Pure Bomb/Mortar players add a non-explosive backup; some fire crosspaths bypass black.

pop_guide.needs: any NON-Cold damage (Sharp, Explosion, Fire, Plasma, Energy, Acid, Normal)
pop_guide.blocked: Cold, Glacier, and Frigid damage cannot pop White (or Zebra) bloons
pop_guide.note: Ice Monkey alone cannot pop White or Zebra.

pop_guide.needs: Sharp, Explosion, Cold, Shatter, Acid, or Normal damage
pop_guide.blocked: Energy, Plasma, Fire, and Frigid damage cannot pop Purple bloons
pop_guide.note: Super Monkey (plasma/energy) and Wizard (fire) struggle into Purple without support; sharp/explosion handle them.

pop_guide.needs: Sharp, Fire, Plasma, Energy, Acid, or Normal damage
pop_guide.blocked: both Explosion AND Cold are blocked (Zebra has Black + White properties)
pop_guide.note: Neither pure bomb nor pure ice works; Sharp or Fire/Plasma does.

pop_guide.needs: any tower with CAMO DETECTION (the damage type is irrelevant to detection)
pop_guide.blocked: towers without camo detection cannot target camo bloons at all
pop_guide.note: Towers with innate camo detection (verified from the game data) are Ninja Monkey, Spike Factory, and Desperado; most others need a crosspath (e.g. Sniper's Night Vision Goggles 0-1-0), Monkey Village Radar Scanner (0-2-0), Alchemist support, or Etienne. Camo is a MODIFIER that can apply to any bloon, not a bloon type.

pop_guide.needs: any NON-Sharp damage (Explosion, Fire, Plasma, Normal)
pop_guide.blocked: Sharp damage cannot pop a Frozen bloon
pop_guide.note: Frozen is a temporary status applied by Ice Monkey, not a permanent property.

pop_guide.needs: any damage type — MOAB-class blimps (MOAB/BFB/ZOMG/BAD) have NO damage-type immunity, only large health
pop_guide.blocked: the DDT is the exception: it carries Lead + Black + Camo, so it resists Sharp/Shatter/Cold/Energy/Explosion and needs camo detection
pop_guide.note: To SLOW/glue/knockback MOAB-class you need MOAB-targeting status upgrades; to DAMAGE a non-DDT blimp any raw damage works. Bonus-vs-MOAB upgrades (e.g. Dartling M.A.D +450 vs MOAB-class) shred them.

pop_guide.needs: camo detection PLUS a damage type that bypasses Lead+Black: Fire, Plasma, Normal, Acid, or Glacier (NOT Sharp, Shatter, Cold, Energy, or Explosion)
pop_guide.blocked: Sharp, Shatter, Cold, Energy, and Explosion are all blocked (Lead + Black); and without camo detection nothing targets it
pop_guide.note: A DDT is fast (400 HP) and MOAB-class with Lead + Black + Camo, so a tower needs THREE things at once: (1) camo detection, (2) a damage type a DDT does not resist — Fire, Plasma, Normal, Acid, or Glacier (NOT Sharp, Shatter, Cold, Energy, or Explosion), AND (3) the ability to damage MOAB-class bloons. Most damage towers satisfy (3), but two important exceptions do NOT until upgraded: base Ice Monkey (its Cold/Glacier attack cannot damage MOAB-class until Embrittlement 4-x-x — so Cold Snap alone, which pops lead+camo, still cannot hit a DDT) and base Glue (needs MOAB Glue 0-0-3). So 'pops lead and sees camo' is necessary but NOT sufficient — the tower must also be able to hit MOAB-class. (The committed stats don't encode MOAB-class targeting, so the bot does not auto-list specific counter towers — recommend by these rules.)
